name: cpu_int_multi
title: CPU integer, all cores
short_description: Integer arithmetic throughput with every core and thread busy.

structured_text.description: Runs the same integer add stream as the single core test, but on every available core and hardware thread at once. The combined result shows how much integer throughput the whole processor can sustain.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Throughput is the total integer operations per second across all cores, divided by one million, reported in Mops/s.
structured_text.value_interpretation: A higher number means more total throughput. The gap between this and the single core result shows how well the chip scales as cores are added.
structured_text.reasoning: All core integer throughput captures the headroom available for parallel workloads such as compiling, encoding and serving many requests at once.

domain_gaming.explanation: Modern engines split work across cores for physics, streaming and draw call submission.

domain_office.explanation: Most office work uses a few cores at most, so extra cores add little.

domain_software_development.explanation: Parallel builds and test suites scale almost directly with available cores.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Some batch filters and exports use several cores, though many edits stay single threaded.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Encoding and effects export spread across all cores during render.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: CPU rendering and simulation bake jobs use every core they can get.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Large sessions distribute tracks across cores, but the real time path stays single threaded.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Meshing and many solvers parallelise across cores during long jobs.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Numerical simulations are routinely threaded across all cores.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Data loading and preprocessing on the host feed the accelerator from many cores.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: CPU side inference and batching benefit from spreading across cores.

domain_data_science.explanation: Group, join and aggregate kernels parallelise across cores over large tables.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Hosting many processes or containers at once depends directly on total core throughput.
